question_title: What will the KOSPI Composite Index closing level be on July 10, 2026?
summary: KOSPI July 10, 2026 forecast centered near the June 29 close (~8,395) with a wide fat-tailed distribution reflecting the current extreme-volatility regime.

factors.description: Current level (June 29 close) is 8,394.65, the resolution anchor; random walk base case.
Extreme realized daily volatility ~4.4-4.8% (annualized ~70%), multiple circuit breakers; ~13-14% one-sigma swing over 9 trading days.
Recent -5.81% reversal plus chip-price sustainability concerns and foreign selling create a mild downside tilt; post-crash momentum is two-sided.

reasoning:
Forecast submitted.

**KOSPI Composite Index — July 10, 2026 close**

- **Median: ~8,292** (anchored on the June 29 close of 8,394.65, with a slight downside tilt)
- **P10–P90: 6,961 – 9,880** (very wide, reflecting the current extreme-volatility regime)
- **P25–P75: 7,579 – 9,071**

**Method:** Monte Carlo (300k paths, Student-t df=4 fat tails) over 9 trading days, using empirical daily σ≈4.5% (annualized ~70%, confirmed by multiple circuit breakers/sidecars in June), with scenario-mixed drift (40% mild-bearish / 40% neutral / 20% bullish) and σ uncertainty. The wide bands are justified — a random-walk one-sigma move alone is ±13-14% over this horizon, and the index recently swung 700+ points intraday in a single session. Tails respect the open bounds (P(<6,500)≈4%, P(>10,500)≈5%).
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the official closing level of the KOSPI Composite Index on July 10, 2026 (Korea Exchange local trading date), as reported in the "Close" column for that date on the Yahoo Finance historical data page for ^KS11: https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EKS11/history/ . Report the value in index points (e.g., a close of 8,411.21 resolves as 8411.21).
fine_print: If July 10, 2026 is not a trading day on the Korea Exchange (e.g., an unscheduled market closure), the question resolves to the official closing level on the most recent prior Korea Exchange trading day on or before July 10, 2026. If the Yahoo Finance historical page is unavailable or does not report a value for the relevant date, the closing level reported by Investing.com (https://www.investing.com/indices/kospi-historical-data) or by other credible sources (https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) for the same date will be used.

factors.description: Current level (June 29 close) is 8,394.65, the resolution anchor. Random walk from here is the base case.
Extreme realized daily volatility ~4.4-4.8% (annualized ~70%), multiple circuit breakers/sidecars. Over 9 trading days this gives ~13-14% one-sigma swing.
Recent sharp reversal (-5.81% on June 26) and chip-price sustainability concerns + foreign selling create mild downside bias, but post-crash momentum is two-sided.

input.anchor: Random walk from current close 8,394.65 with empirical daily σ~4.5% over 9 trading days.
input.assessment: Short-horizon (9 trading days) index forecast. Anchor on current close 8,394.65. Extremely high volatility regime (daily σ~4.5%, recent circuit breakers). Used Monte Carlo with Student-t (df=4) fat tails, scenario-mixed drift (40% mild bearish, 40% neutral, 20% bullish) and σ uncertainty (0.038/0.045/0.055). Median 8,292, P10 6,961, P90 9,880. Tails: P(<6500)=4.3%, P(>10500)=4.7%, consistent with open bounds. Width matches random-walk implied range — not narrower. Distribution roughly symmetric in log space, slight downside tilt from recent reversal.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether the bubble-like rally resumes or the crash deepens; circuit-breaker tail events; chip-price trajectory.
input.tool_audit: web_search gave current quote across CNBC/TradingEconometrics/Yahoo (June 29 close ~8,394.65, confirmed). Yahoo chart API in sandbox returned clean 3mo daily series. execute_code for MC worked.
input.process_reflection: Smooth for a short-horizon index question. Direct Yahoo chart API fetch in sandbox is reliable and avoids HTML scraping. No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all three factors against the trace; all core claims are grounded.

FACTOR CHECKS:
- Factor 1 (current close 8,394.65 as anchor): Confirmed by CNBC quote ("8,394.65 -16.56 (-0.20%)") and the Yahoo chart API last row (2026-06-29 8394.65). Logit +0.0 (anchor) correct.
- Factor 2 (daily vol ~4.4-4.8%, annualized ~70%, circuit breakers/sidecars): Confirmed — trace computed full-window σ=0.0442, last15=0.0477, last10=0.0468, annualized=0.70. Circuit breaker/sidecar claim supported by TradingView snippet ("5 sidecars and one circuit breaker... June 23... almost 10%"). Logit +0.0 (width) correct.
- Factor 3 (-5.81% reversal on June 26): Verified arithmetically: June 25 close 8930.3 → June 26 close 8411.21 = -5.81%. Logit -0.2 (mild downside) consistent with description.

NO ERRORS FOUND in the higher-risk categories:
- Regime-spanning / contaminated drift: The agent correctly did NOT use the empirical historical mean drift (+0.39%/day, a bubble artifact that would have been a regime error). It used a scenario-mixed drift instead, producing a sensible slightly-bearish median. Volatility was blended across windows and given uncertainty (0.038/0.045/0.055), appropriately handling the regime.
- Distribution calibration: Median 8,292, P10 6,961, P90 9,880. With σ~4.5%/day over 9 days (1σ log ≈ 13.5%), the ±1σ band is ~7,335–9,608; the wider P10/P90 reflect Student-t(df=4) fat tails and σ-mixing — appropriate for a confirmed extreme-vol regime. Internally consistent and consistent with the open bounds.
- Counterargument: Strong and genuinely two-sided (vol may normalize → too wide; OR foreign selling/chip concerns persist → median too high), with concrete falsifiers. Not a token gesture.
- Self-confidence 0.60: Well-calibrated for a 9-day horizon in this regime; not overconfident.
- Tool audit narrative matches ground-truth metrics (3 execute_code, 2 web_search, 1 reflection, 0 errors; no false "no failures" claim).
- Worldview consistency: Only related entry (SK Hynix volatility, June 2-3) is archived/stale and broadly consistent (high vol, foreign selling). No KOSPI numeric sub-forecast exists to create a CDF inconsistency. No contradictions.
- Resolution alignment: Correct ticker (^KS11), correct source family (Yahoo), forward-looking question (no pre-publication event issue). Aligned.

INDEPENDENT ESTIMATE: Spot 8,395; near-spot median with slight downside tilt and very wide fat-tailed bounds is exactly what the evidence supports. My estimate agrees with the agent's center (~8,292) and range (6,961–9,880).

Minor (non-material) note: the ~1.2% sub-spot median is driven by the assumed bearish drift mixture rather than hard directional evidence — a pure random walk would center at 8,395. This shift is within noise and is explicitly acknowledged in the counterargument, so it does not warrant a downgrade.
